response.justification: This candidate is a highly accomplished software engineer with 17+ years of professional experience, but a fundamentally poor fit for this role. The position mandates 5–10 years of professional C++ on Windows with capital markets or banking domain expertise. The resume shows only ~6 months of professional C++ work (at National Instruments), no Windows platform experience, and zero exposure to fixed income, derivatives, or investment banking systems. While strong in web, mobile, and systems programming, the candidate lacks the specific technical foundation and domain knowledge this role requires.
strengths: 17+ years of professional software development across diverse domains and platforms demonstrates mature engineering fundamentals
Excellent communication and mentoring skills evidenced by multiple workshops, talks, and teaching engagements
Proven rapid learning ability and adaptability across languages, frameworks, and industries
concerns: Only ~6 months of documented professional C++ experience when 5–10 years is a mandatory requirement
No evidence of Windows platform expertise or SQL Server/Oracle database experience; background is primarily Node.js, TypeScript, and DLang on Linux
Complete absence of capital markets, fixed income, derivatives, investment banking, or financial services domain expertise
